data.narrative:
Aalo Atomics is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Matt Loszak that has raised $136M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 11 peers across the nuclear sector—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Antares.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $136M total raised proxy, reflecting typical valuation expansion between Series B and the next funding stage. This method yields an implied valuation of $258M. A peer-median approach is not applicable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none meet the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints when filtered by stage, adjacent comparable, or valuation tier. Deploying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given nuclear energy's specialized capital intensity and regulatory environment.

The triangulated valuation range is $220M to $297M, with a median of $258M. This single-method result carries meaningful uncertainty. The wide range ($77M spread) reflects the sparsity of directly comparable Series B nuclear companies and the nascent stage of the sector's public market data. Key uncertainties include the degree to which recent public market activity in nuclear energy will sustain valuations and the extent to which Aalo's specific technology, team, and capital efficiency differ from identified peers. Additional clarity on unit economics and regulatory pathway would strengthen conviction in any point estimate within this range.
